Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.group

Class FlixelGroup<T>

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.group.FlixelGroup<T>
Type Parameters:
T - Member type (unconstrained).
All Implemented Interfaces:
FlixelGroupable<T>
public class FlixelGroup<T> extends Object implements FlixelGroupable<T>
Framework-agnostic member list backed by a SnapshotArray. Use this in a plain libGDX Screen or game loop with any member type (FlixelGroup<Actor>, FlixelGroup<YourEntity>, etc.): call add(T), remove(T), and FlixelGroupable.forEachMember(java.util.function.Consumer<T>) yourself; there is no automatic update/draw.

For FlixelGDX objects that extend FlixelBasic, use FlixelBasicGroup or FlixelState instead so members receive the usual update/draw/recycle lifecycle.

See Also:
  • FlixelBasicGroup

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    FlixelGroup(@NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.ArraySupplier<T[]> arrayFactory)
    Creates a new group with the given array factory and unlimited size by default.
    FlixelGroup(@NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.ArraySupplier<T[]> arrayFactory, int maxSize)
    Creates a new group with the given array factory and maximum size.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    add(T member)
    Adds a member to this group.
    void
    clear()
    Removes all members from the group without touching member instances.
    void
    ensureMembers()
     
    int
    getMaxSize()
    Returns the maximum number of members allowed.
    @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.utils.SnapshotArray<T>
    getMembers()
    Returns the backing array, or null if the implementation has not allocated it yet (FlixelGroup).
    void
    remove(T member)
    Removes the member from this group only.
    void
    resetStorage()
    Clears the member list and discards the backing SnapshotArray so the next ensureMembers() or add(T) allocates a fresh array.
    void
    setMaxSize(int maxSize)
    Sets the maximum number of members allowed.

      Creates a new group with the given array factory and unlimited size by default.
      Parameters:
      arrayFactory - The array factory to use.

    • FlixelGroup

      public FlixelGroup(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.ArraySupplier<T[]> arrayFactory, int maxSize)
      Creates a new group with the given array factory and maximum size.
      Parameters:
      arrayFactory - The array factory to use.
      maxSize - The maximum size of the group.

  • Method Details

    • ensureMembers

      public void ensureMembers()

    • add

      public void add(T member)
      Description copied from interface: FlixelGroupable
      Adds a member to this group.
      Specified by:
      add in interface FlixelGroupable<T>

    • remove

      public void remove(T member)
      Description copied from interface: FlixelGroupable
      Removes the member from this group only. Does not interpret or tear down the member; see FlixelBasicGroupable for optional destroy semantics on FlixelBasic members.
      Specified by:
      remove in interface FlixelGroupable<T>

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Description copied from interface: FlixelGroupable
      Removes all members from the group without touching member instances.
      Specified by:
      clear in interface FlixelGroupable<T>

    • resetStorage

      public void resetStorage()
      Clears the member list and discards the backing SnapshotArray so the next ensureMembers() or add(T) allocates a fresh array. Does not call any method on member instances.

    • getMembers

      @Nullable public @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.utils.SnapshotArray<T> getMembers()
      Description copied from interface: FlixelGroupable
      Returns the backing array, or null if the implementation has not allocated it yet (FlixelGroup).
      Specified by:
      getMembers in interface FlixelGroupable<T>

    • getMaxSize

      public int getMaxSize()
      Description copied from interface: FlixelGroupable
      Returns the maximum number of members allowed. When 0, the group can grow without limit.
      Specified by:
      getMaxSize in interface FlixelGroupable<T>

    • setMaxSize

      public void setMaxSize(int maxSize)
      Description copied from interface: FlixelGroupable
      Sets the maximum number of members allowed. Values less than 0 are clamped to 0 (unlimited).
      Specified by:
      setMaxSize in interface FlixelGroupable<T>
